description_long: This object is a heavy-duty sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular black metal head and a solid wooden handle. The head is thick and blocky, featuring a flat striking face on one end and a slightly tapered, wedge-like back on the other. The handle is cylindrical, made of wood with a natural, slightly weathered finish, and appears to be securely fitted into the head. The wood grain is visible, and the handle is straight with no visible grip enhancements or ergonomic shaping. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and force over decorative elements. This tool is commonly used for demolition, breaking concrete, or driving stakes in urban construction or maintenance settings.
description: A heavy sledgehammer with a black metal head and a straight wooden handle, designed for demolition or heavy-duty striking tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the rectangular black metal head with a flat striking face.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow face of the metal head and the circular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the wedge-like back of the head and the straight handle.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the head and the handle's attachment point.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a natural finish, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular, blocky metal head with a flat striking face and a slightly tapered back; no sharp edges or beard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the head.
